taskDescription: Click on the key neuroanatomical terms related to the pons in the text below.
textField:
The *pons* is divided into a ventral *basis* *pontis* and a dorsal *tegmentum*. The basis pontis contains *transverse* *pontine* fibres and scattered *pontine* *nuclei* that relay cortical input to the *cerebellum* via the *middle* *cerebellar* *peduncle*.

The *facial* nerve (CN *VII*) fibres loop over the *abducens* nucleus (CN *VI*) to form the *genu* of CN VII, creating the *facial* *colliculus* on the floor of the *fourth* *ventricle*. The *trigeminal* nerve (CN *V*) emerges from the lateral aspect of the pons.

In *locked-in* *syndrome* caused by *pontine* *haemorrhage*, the patient is fully *conscious* but can only communicate through *vertical* eye movements. The *locus* *coeruleus* in the pons is the principal source of *noradrenaline* projections to the cortex. The *pneumotaxic* centre in the pons helps regulate the rhythm of *breathing*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the key neuroanatomical terms of the pons.
Good effort! You identified many key terms.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of the terminology.
